title: Let's talk art: What's on at the theatres
description: GEORGE NEWS - For one night only, on Sunday...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - For one night only, on Sunday 23 February, the award-winning comedian, presenter, writer and actor, Dr Riaad Moosa, returns to the stage with his thought-provoking and hilarious new show What's the Point? at George Arts Theatre (15:00).

Known as "The Comedy Doctor", Riaad combines his sharp comedic wit and medical insights to delve into life’s big (and small) questions. The show starts at 15:00 and tickets are available on Quicket (R200). Age restriction: no children under 14.

Check the schedule below for top notch entertainment in George for the rest of February and early March:


> 21 February


Get ready to warm up those vocal cords at the George Arts Theatre's karaoke evening.

Everyone is welcome to come and sing their heart out.

Entry fees: R20 for non-members and free for active members. The bar will be open, so grab a drink, grab the mic, and make it a night to remember!


> 26 February to 8 March


Memoirs of a Chocolate Factory (19:00 with Saturday matinees 14:00) at George Arts Theatre. A vibrant, witty, and heartwarming musical that reimagines the beloved characters of the classic chocolate factory story - now grown up and facing a very different world. Tickets cost R120 (members R100) and are available through Computicket.

Ché de Bruyn as Gizmo, one of the Oompa Loompas in Roald Dahl's famous children's story. Book tickets now on Computicket for Memoirs of a Chocolate Factory, showcased at George Arts Theatre.


> 27 February


Rockin' All Over the World (19:00) at Market Theatre. Saxophonist Andrew Young tells stories of his global travels through music, accompanied by Cape Town pianist Tony Drake. Tickets cost R160 and is only available on his website, www.andrewyoung.net.


> 6 Maart


Joodse feesmaal vir Fynproewers (09:00) by die Bahia Bleu-restaurant by die Diaz-hotel. Die Suid-Kaap Fynproewersgilde word getrakteer deur Tracy Klass van A Taste of Klass-faam in die wondere van die Joodse kookkuns.

Klass, ook ‘n bekende komediant, kom haar liefde vir kos met Fynproewers deel. Dit kom al die pad van haar twee Joodse tantes wat in Litaue gewoon het af. Die wonderlikste Joodse smake, kleure en geure gaan gaste betower. Die dag beloof om ‘n heerlike ondervinding te wees. Bespreek by Ena Jacobs by 082 416 5304.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
